name: PyTorch MPS
text:
To solve f(x) + f(y) = f(x + y - xy) with f(1) = 1, first check whether the assumptions are consistent.

Set x = 1. Then
f(1) + f(y) = f(1 + y - y) = f(1).

Using f(1) = 1, this becomes
1 + f(y) = 1,
so f(y) = 0 for every real y.

But taking y = 1 gives f(1) = 0, contradicting the condition f(1) = 1. Equivalently, setting x = y = 1 gives 1 + 1 = f(1), i.e. 2 = 1.

Therefore the given conditions are inconsistent. There is no function f satisfying the statement, so the requested integer set is not well-defined.

Final answer: no such function exists.
